trivia: The stretched CRJ brought 70 seats to the regional jet boom. Faster boarding, small airports, hub feed: it defined a whole tier of North American flying alongside the E170.
Long, low and slim with tail-mounted engines: the CRJ silhouette is unmistakable even at cruise.
funFacts: Derived from the Challenger business jet fuselage cross-section.
Hundreds still feed major US hubs daily.
